design_philosophy.core_loop: Side-scrolling beat 'em up combat against waves of criminals and themed boss villains, with ability to summon Marvel hero allies for specific encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing limited moveset while maintaining aggression against enemy variety; deciding when to summon aid versus saving allies for harder encounters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as Spider-Man or Venom as part of a Marvel resistance movement, teaming up with iconic heroes (Captain America, Iron Fist, Black Cat, etc.) to defeat Carnage and his criminal empire
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict adherence to comic book narrative source material (Maximum Carnage story arc); direct use of comic panels for cutscenes rather than original storytelling; character-specific ally effectiveness (heroes favor Spider-Man or Venom differently); allies summoned through environmental discovery rather than earned progression

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives-based system typical of beat 'em up genre
difficulty.continues_system: Continues available; specific continue count not documented
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with fixed level structure
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Low; limited moveset creates execution ceiling. Players can optimize by discovering ally locations (hidden power-ups set in specific level locations), but core difficulty determined by enemy patterns and character animation frames

progression.unlock_mechanism: Hidden power-up items in fixed level locations unlock summoned hero allies; each summoned hero provides temporary combat assistance with character-dependent effectiveness
replayability_hooks: Two playable characters (Spider-Man and Venom) with different ally synergies
Discovery of hidden ally summoning items encourages level re-exploration
Character-specific ally effectiveness creates tactical variation between playthroughs

world_building_techniques: Semi-animated comic panel cutscenes (art taken directly from source comics)
Story follows Marvel's Maximum Carnage comic arc faithfully
Villain hierarchy established through narrative (Carnage recruits Shriek, Demogoblin, Doppelganger, Carrion)
Environmental storytelling through level design (sewers as Carnage's criminal empire base, city-wide incursion)
Character arc: Eddie Brock/Venom's redemption journey and Spider-Man/Venom rivalry-turned-alliance

boss_design.philosophy: Thematic villain encounters tied directly to comic narrative; each boss represents Carnage's recruited team members with distinct visual identity; final boss (Carnage himself) represents climactic confrontation of resistance versus criminal empire

faction_indicators: Carnage's criminal empire (sewers as power base)
Hobgoblin's rejection leading to Demogoblin transformation (outcasting mechanic)
Carnage's hierarchy: Shriek as lieutenant, enforcer team (Doppelganger, Demogoblin, Carrion)
danger_signals: Symbiote corruption (Carnage as uncontrollable threat despite Brock's promise to reform)
Criminal wave spreading through city (hundreds of inspired criminals following Carnage)
Symbiote escape at narrative end (threat persistence despite victory)
environmental_storytelling: Sewer levels as criminal empire infrastructure
City-wide Carnage incursion showing widespread criminal activity
Hero recruitment mechanics showing desperate alliance-building
Ally summoning through hidden discovery (grassroots resistance gathering resources)

sources_used: Wikipedia: Spider-Man and Venom: Maximum Carnage
